127835.explanation: The municipal government should be a good reflection of the people living in Zoetermeer. With an equal number of men and women, so that all perspectives are represented to make fair decisions.

127836.explanation: As residents, we govern our own municipality. When an issue plays a major role, a referendum can be a fair and effective way to make a decision. That way, we involve people in local democracy.

127839.explanation: People fleeing war and violence deserve a safe place. We do take concerns about housing and the neighborhood seriously. The asylum seeker center will remain open, but we ensure it remains fair and livable for everyone.

127846.explanation: Sports give a lot of energy and satisfaction. And it keeps you healthy. But for people with welfare benefits, sports are often too expensive. We believe the municipality should ensure this group can participate in a sports club for free.

127847.explanation: Local media are extremely important to know what is happening in your own area, from the street to the municipal council. But many local media struggle to keep afloat. We believe the municipality should contribute to this.

127849.explanation: Children are under huge pressure, from addictive social media to performance pressure at school. Music is the perfect outlet. We want music lessons not only to be accessible to children with wealthy parents. This way, we offer every child equal opportunities.

127852.explanation: Boa’s are indispensable for a safe and pleasant neighborhood. They are getting busier, so we want more boa’s. We also continue to advocate for more neighborhood police officers. This way we can make and keep neighborhoods safe.

127853.explanation: Feeling safe on the street is very important. But more cameras are not the right solution in our opinion. Your privacy is a great asset that should not be violated lightly. Therefore, we prefer more street enforcement officers who can intervene immediately if something goes wrong.

127854.explanation: Boa’s are indispensable for a safe neighborhood. They contribute to the livability of the city and maintain contact within the community. We provide the boa’s with the proper tools and training needed for this. We see the work of the police as truly different from that of boa’s.

127855.explanation: Buying a first home is often unaffordable in Zoetermeer. We want to help first-time buyers with a special loan from the municipality. This way, you can borrow just a bit extra on top of your mortgage. This gives young people and starters a fair chance at their own place again.

127856.explanation: We can already do this. The housing shortage cannot be solved by priority alone but by building more houses. We must not pit groups against each other. We must build extra social housing for all households so that the 'Zoetermeer residents first' policy is no longer necessary, or at most remains supplemental.

127857.explanation: The housing market is completely stalled, largely because older people cannot move to a different or smaller home. Therefore, we want to build homes of that type as soon as possible, especially affordable ones. This will free up homes again for other house seekers.

127861.explanation: People are not on welfare because they don’t want to work. They need a stable foundation, affordable housing, and trust from the municipality. Extra demands and conditions only create more uncertainty and cause new problems.

127865.explanation: We want to experiment with a basic income as soon as the law allows it. People would then receive a fixed amount without mandatory reciprocity. We choose trust instead of strict controls. This gives residents peace and the chance to really move forward.

127866.explanation: Zoetermeer already has many charging stations in the neighborhoods. We will not sacrifice street parking spaces lightly. We prefer to choose smart charging plazas on large parking lots. This way, space remains in the neighborhood and electric cars can still charge.

127867.explanation: The figures clearly show: a lower maximum speed makes traffic in our municipality much safer. For cycling children and elderly people with reduced mobility, this makes a huge difference. Moreover, the streets become quieter and the air cleaner.

127870.explanation: We only introduce permits if there is really too little parking space in a neighborhood. We do want to link parking costs to public transport. In places with good public transport connections, you pay a bit more. This encourages green choices.

127871.explanation: Care is about people. You have to feel safe and understood by your care provider. That is why we find it important that there is room for your faith or culture. Care in Zoetermeer must be accessible and personal for everyone, whoever you are.

127873.explanation: No one should have stress about money for tampons or sanitary pads. These are basic necessities, not luxuries. We provide free products at schools and community centers. We have already submitted multiple proposals for this in the municipal council.

127876.explanation: Zoetermeer is not Amsterdam; an increase yields little here and we have already done so in recent years. We prefer visitors to co-pay rather than burden our own residents, but this does not solve our financial worries. We are looking for smart solutions that truly help the budget.

127877.explanation: Our space is limited and we love the greenery. We do not expand at the expense of nature. We prefer to improve and make existing sites more sustainable. We also see if we can build homes on top of commercial buildings. We even return part of the Prisma industrial estate to greenery.

127878.explanation: We find it important that shops can be open on Sundays for shopkeepers and customers who want that. We are already used to this. It also makes it much easier for working people to do their shopping.

127879.explanation: The housing shortage is the real problem. We refuse to pit house seekers against each other. People fleeing war need a safe place, just like starters. The solution is not fighting over scarcity but building hard for everyone.

127881.explanation: A field full of solar panels can provide many households with clean, affordable energy. If farmers want, they should be able to use their land for this. Moreover, solar panels can be well combined with more biodiversity, for example by growing flowers and other low greenery.

127883.explanation: The Netherlands is a water country and every child should learn to swim. Including children whose parents cannot afford swimming lessons. Unfortunately, this is still not fairly arranged. The best solution: school swimming for every child, with support from the municipality.

127885.explanation: We prefer sales in a shop with rules over illegal dealers on the street. Currently, the supply is too limited with only one shop. By allowing a new shop, we distribute the crowds and maintain oversight of safety and health.

127886.explanation: We raise the bar even higher: we want at least 35 percent social rental housing in new construction. Housing is a right, not a luxury. The waiting lists are far too long. We must build for our teachers, police officers, and young people, not for the large profits of project developers.

127888.explanation: We have a huge housing shortage, and many people in our municipality live more spaciously than necessary. Sharing a house helps against the housing shortage. But the neighborhood should not experience nuisance. We are strict against exploitation: people may not be hidden away in expensive, poor-quality rooms.

127889.explanation: A strong neighborhood needs a heart. We want a place in every neighborhood where residents can meet each other. That is also the best medicine against loneliness. Moreover, you can turn to this place for help or activities close to home.

127891.explanation: Space in the neighborhood is precious. We choose green, trees, and playgrounds instead of more pavement for stationary cars. We prefer to reduce parking pressure by offering good alternatives, such as good public transport, shared cars, and fast bike paths.

127893.explanation: SnowWorld is valuable for the city, but the Buytenpark deserves protection. Growth on its own grounds is possible, but expansion into green areas or new attractions like the zipline are undesirable. Balance between recreation and nature is essential.

127895.explanation: We want to move off gas quickly for a better climate. 2040 is ambitious but necessary. We start with savings, for example by insulating. That is the cheapest option for your wallet and the climate. The municipality must ensure this is affordable for everyone. No one should be left out in the cold.

127896.explanation: A district heating network can help to go off gas. But we have conditions: it must be affordable and truly sustainable. Insulation remains step one. What you don't consume, you don't have to pay for.

127897.explanation: It is important that we separate waste better to take good care of the environment and keep costs affordable for our residents. GroenLinks-PvdA chooses the method that best suits this. This does not seem to involve charging for residual waste.

127898.explanation: Everyone should be able to get home safely after a night out in Rotterdam. Currently, public transport often stops too early. A night bus is a safe and affordable solution. This way you are not dependent on an expensive taxi or car. In the future, we also want a rail connection to Rotterdam.

127835.explanation: We choose leaders based on their qualities.

127836.explanation: We believe in good consultation with residents, but not in expensive and slow referenda.

127839.explanation: The asylum intake in the Netherlands must be reduced. However, the municipality is not responsible for this. We believe our municipality should do the minimum legally required. Not less, but absolutely not more.

127846.explanation: However, we always want to offer children in vulnerable situations perspective: every child must be able to participate in sports, school, and cultural activities, regardless of their home situation.

127847.explanation: Good and independent media are important, and it is good that the municipality contributes to this. The current budget does not need to be increased for that.

127849.explanation: However, we always want to offer children in vulnerable situations perspective: every child must be able to participate in sports, school, and cultural activities, regardless of their home situation.

127852.explanation: We want to increase the number of enforcement officers (boa’s) further from 28 to 42.

127853.explanation: To prevent crime and increase the chance of catching offenders, cameras are deployed in strategic locations.

127854.explanation: The people who work for our safety must have good equipment and be able to defend themselves if necessary.

127855.explanation: The only solution to the housing shortage is to build more homes. We are working hard on that. Starter loans cost the municipality a lot of money and cause higher house prices.

127856.explanation: Especially healthcare workers, police officers, teachers, firefighters, etc. should get priority for social rental housing. It doesn’t matter whether you come from inside or outside Zoetermeer.

127857.explanation: We don’t have enough homes; we can only solve this by building more. To help as many people as possible to find suitable housing, flow-through is needed. We do this by deploying flow-through brokers and by building specifically for people who are currently stuck, such as empty nesters and seniors.

127861.explanation: Actively guiding residents towards paid work or education is the best way to fight poverty and the key to independence. Fewer benefits, more participation. Therefore, we find it logical that people with social assistance benefits provide a counter-service to society where possible.

127866.explanation: If there is demand from the neighborhood. We have also made it easy for people to charge themselves (via a charging cable).

127867.explanation: The best way to prevent traffic casualties is by designing roads to match the speed limit, not just by replacing speed signs. In a residential area where you must drive at walking pace, there should be no space to speed through the street.

127870.explanation: Free parking is the norm. Paid parking is only used as a last resort in case of parking problems. We encourage two hours of free parking in the city center.

127871.explanation: If there are possibilities for it, people should be able to express a preference where they feel comfortable. We are against discrimination based on religion, culture, or belief.

127873.explanation: People in financial need receive benefits, compensation, or can turn to institutions for help.

127876.explanation: We have recently increased the tourist tax and this has not led to fewer tourists.

127877.explanation: There is no need for expansion, and we are actually looking at various locations to transform business parks into residential neighborhoods. We invest in the existing business parks and maintain them well.

127878.explanation: The choice to be open on Sundays is up to the entrepreneur.

127879.explanation: We abolish the preferential position of migrants with a residence permit for social rental housing. We only provide temporary housing in the former prison.

127881.explanation: But only in combination with investments in the surrounding nature.

127883.explanation: Parents in Zoetermeer receive a contribution so their children can take swimming lessons.

127885.explanation: Zoetermeer has only one coffeeshop, which is few for a city of 130,000 inhabitants.

127886.explanation: We don’t have enough homes; we can only solve this by building more. We have enough social rental homes, but too many people who are no longer entitled to them are stuck there. Therefore, we focus on flow-through from social housing.

127888.explanation: We are against subdividing houses into multiple small units. This often causes extra nuisance, parking pressure, and a decline in livability in streets and neighborhoods. We want to protect livability and focus on real housing solutions.

127889.explanation: Meeting places for neighborhood residents are important, especially as central places where people can easily meet and organize activities. We build on the success of the Integrated Child Centers (IKCs) and strengthen their role as meeting and development places.

127891.explanation: We ensure enough parking spaces with new construction. Where residents indicate a greater need for more parking spaces, we will cooperate.

127893.explanation: SnowWorld is an important attraction for our city and plans submitted to the municipality must be taken seriously and thoroughly investigated.

127895.explanation: Sustainability is a marathon, not a sprint. We focus on achieving the sustainability goals of the national government. Where possible, we take extra steps, but we do not unnecessarily impose costs on residents to be a 'front-runner.'

127896.explanation: Due to high costs, Zoetermeer has stopped participating in the regional district heating network. We stimulate the sustainability of buildings in the city and ensure that what we ask and do is achievable and affordable.

127897.explanation: We are against introducing diftar, where you have to pay per time you open the underground container or per time you put the waste container at the curb.

127898.explanation: You must also be able to return home from Rotterdam in the evening. We would even prefer a direct metro or tram to Rotterdam, the so-called ZoRo line. We will continue working on extending metro line D between Centrum West and Rotterdam Central (via Lansingerland-Zoetermeer station).

127835.explanation: With us, the choice for quality takes precedence over gender.

127836.explanation: The choice has already been made through elections. We do not want to unnecessarily burden residents with the complexity of policy. Referendums often have low turnout, meaning a small group decides.

127839.explanation: LHN believes Zoetermeer fulfills its legal obligation but does not organize more shelter than strictly necessary. The city does what it must, but extra voluntary expansion of shelter capacity is not desirable. The focus is on capacity, safety, and livability for all residents.

127846.explanation: In Zoetermeer, we have the ZoetermeerPass. This allows people on social assistance to choose for themselves how to spend it. This enables everyone to participate in the city.

127847.explanation: In consultation with the local broadcaster, an assessment should be made of what is needed to maintain the broadcaster within the statutory budget.

127849.explanation: In Zoetermeer, we have the ZoetermeerPass. This allows people on social assistance to choose for themselves how to spend it. This enables everyone to participate in the city.

127852.explanation: LHN wants a 24-hour network of community enforcement officers (BOAs) and enforcement. More deployment of BOAs in areas where nuisance occurs. Close cooperation with police, youth work, and neighborhood teams. Quick and clear feedback to residents and entrepreneurs.

127853.explanation: In locations where nuisance occurs or is suspected, we want the mayor to take earlier action to safeguard the feeling of safety.

127854.explanation: A topic that has come up for LHN for years. Our enforcement officers must be able to defend themselves. The tasks are becoming heavier; the equipment must grow accordingly.

127855.explanation: A starter loan gives first-time buyers a financial boost to buy a home otherwise out of reach. Zoetermeer benefits as it retains young residents in the city, stimulates housing flow, and keeps the local housing market healthy.

127856.explanation: Those rooted in Zoetermeer deserve a fair chance at housing. Therefore, LHN wants Zoetermeerders with proven ties to the city to get priority for social rental homes. As soon as national regulations allow, we want to extend this to owner-occupied homes and broader groups.

127857.explanation: Seniors are connected to their neighborhood and network. LHN wants to give them the opportunity to continue living in their familiar environment. More ground-floor homes in the neighborhood; senior living communities in the districts; preservation of care facilities and meeting places.

127861.explanation: Everyone contributes their part, from volunteer work to a learning-development pathway toward paid employment. This stimulates participation, self-esteem, and offers perspective, exactly what strengthens our society.

127865.explanation: A basic income separates income from work. That sounds liberating. Work is not only money but also structure, social contacts, purpose, and development. If the incentive to work diminishes, it may lead to less labor market participation.

127866.explanation: There are enough charging stations. These must grow along with demand. The municipality should especially facilitate charging for residents with solar panels.

127867.explanation: The speed limit should only be adjusted in places where it contributes to traffic safety. For example, when housing or other developments require a lower speed.

127870.explanation: In neighborhoods where parking nuisance occurs, for example due to offices or outsiders, we think this is a good idea.

127871.explanation: Care is obviously a personal choice. At the same time, we have a collective responsibility. When the choice is not obvious, responsibility for care takes precedence.

127873.explanation: According to LHN, this does not fall under the core tasks of the municipality, such as education, safety, and infrastructure. Furthermore, this would shortchange others who cannot afford other care products, such as diapers.

127876.explanation: We must ensure that Zoetermeer becomes more attractive to tourists. The more tourists, the more tax revenue we collect in total. This does not require raising the rate.

127877.explanation: Space is limited. We must handle it smarter. Business parks in Zoetermeer can still be densified well. Also consider mixed-use. Space could be allocated to hospitality, housing, and education without companies losing business park area.

127878.explanation: We want to be a vibrant city, with room for entrepreneurship, conviviality, and accessible amenities.

127879.explanation: Housing must be fairly distributed, without undermining residents' sense of justice. Not because we deny people in need a roof over their heads, but because it damages residents’ sense of fairness.

127881.explanation: Despite the sustainable benefits, LHN sees disadvantages. Especially the large land use, impact on landscape and nature, and dependence on variable sunlight. LHN prefers solar panels on roofs, both municipal properties and private ownership.

127883.explanation: We live in a water-rich country. It is important that children learn to swim, just as naturally as speaking the Dutch language.

127885.explanation: More points of sale lead to more visibility, availability, and therefore more nuisance. Currently, it is limited to one manageable sales point.

127886.explanation: There are neighborhoods in Zoetermeer where the social rental percentage exceeds 45 percent. LHN looks at what each neighborhood needs. Sometimes that means less social rent in a new project, sometimes a bit more than 30 percent. LHN strives for a maximum of 30 percent social rent citywide.

127888.explanation: This way, we create homes without building one extra square meter. It results in affordable starter or senior housing, with clear rules on affordability and livability.

127889.explanation: LHN focuses on Community Houses. This may be an existing library, community center, or neighborhood garden. This gives young and old a warm place to meet, do activities together, and prevent loneliness.

127891.explanation: In neighborhoods where we build for young families and seniors, this certainly warrants attention. We must use scarce space smarter. Green patches can sometimes be converted into green parking spots, especially if it improves street livability and safety.

127893.explanation: SnowWorld is of great added value to Zoetermeer as a leading leisure company offering year-round winter sports experiences. This creates jobs and contributes to the local economy. Additionally, investments are made in greenery in the Buytenpark.

127895.explanation: It is allowed. Not everyone wants to become gas-free, and that is understandable. But no one wants a drafty home, high bills, or dependence on a single energy source. So let's have a conversation about comfort, freedom of choice, and affordability, not coercion.

127896.explanation: A regional heating network is not an end in itself. It is a smart solution in dense neighborhoods and new developments. But we will not impose it in streets where it is technically or financially illogical. Residents must keep freedom of choice, in comfort and financially.

127897.explanation: The polluter pays. And we already do that. Waste collection fees are charged per number of people in a household.

127898.explanation: A city that takes young people seriously ensures they get home safely and affordably. Following the night bus between Zoetermeer and Leiden, we think this is a good idea.

127835.explanation: The PDvZ believes that expertise and quality should be the main points for the council. If, in addition, gender balance is achieved based on suitability, that is even better.

127836.explanation: A referendum is an excellent tool to hear residents’ opinions on special issues. However, the careful determination of what can and cannot be asked via referendum must be very precise.

127839.explanation: Within Zoetermeer, there are no asylum seekers but only status holders. People who have truly fled war or oppression must be able to find a safe place in Zoetermeer. Economic refugees are not welcome here.

127846.explanation: Zoetermeer has schemes (such as the ZoetermeerPass) that arrange this kind of membership in sports clubs or other associations for people on social assistance. So the municipality already does this.

127847.explanation: A local broadcaster has a social function. It is good that this is subsidized by the municipality. Quality can therefore be expected for the residents.

127849.explanation: A good idea but not necessary. The municipality offers the ZoetermeerPas for students whose parents cannot afford extra lessons. Other parents can pay for the lessons. And not every student wants to take music lessons.

127852.explanation: Several enforcement officers (boa's) have recently been added. Naturally, we want more boa's to increase safety.

127853.explanation: In places where it is really necessary (due to nuisance or threats), camera surveillance is an excellent means to increase safety.

127854.explanation: Equipping boa's with a baton is a good idea, provided that the baton is only used in extreme cases. This also prevents escalation from occurring too quickly during an incident.

127855.explanation: We are strong supporters of this. For first-time buyers, it offers the chance to finally own a home, and for elderly people living in houses that are too large, it allows them to purchase an age-appropriate home. This also promotes housing market flow.

127856.explanation: The PDvZ has believed this for years. However, due to the system used in Haaglanden (Woonnet), this is impossible. Yet people prefer to continue living in their own municipality.

127857.explanation: Elderly people who currently live in too large a home but cannot (or will not) move can more easily obtain a smaller home. This ensures a good flow in the housing market.

127861.explanation: It is not always possible for people receiving benefits to work (due to disabilities). But a counter-performance may be required from those who have been unemployed for a long time and receive benefits.

127865.explanation: The PDvZ believes this is a matter for the national government, not the municipality. The municipality has no resources for this. The national government does have those resources.

127866.explanation: There is already a major shortage of parking spots in neighborhoods. We do not support designating even more spots for electric charging. Sales of electric cars are also declining, so the necessity is not present.

127867.explanation: Maximum speed should only be reduced near schools. Zoetermeer’s layout ensures cyclists and pedestrians are well separated from other traffic. There is no need to implement a 30 km/h speed limit throughout the entire neighborhood. Enforcement of speed limits is important though.

127870.explanation: The PDvZ is strongly opposed to parking permits. Everyone should be able to park their car in designated spots. Permits drive visitors away and only lead to higher costs for residents.

127871.explanation: We live in a country where everyone is free to think and express their opinions. That people are allowed, based on their faith or beliefs, to choose who provides their care is a right.

127873.explanation: For girls and women, it is important to have access to these products when needed. Some people simply cannot afford them. The PDvZ is a strong supporter of assistance.

127876.explanation: Tourist tax may only be increased according to the index. Not many tourists come to Zoetermeer, so any increase is negligible as a total amount.

127877.explanation: The PDvZ wants a reorganization of the business parks. Expansion is not possible because Zoetermeer has no more land. But business parks should be relocated to the outskirts of the municipality to free up space for housing.

127878.explanation: Sunday shopping has been established for so long it is now indispensable. Entrepreneurs should have the freedom to decide whether to open or close on Sundays.

127879.explanation: The PDvZ advocates for equality in housing allocation. Residents on waiting lists for social rental housing should not be disadvantaged compared to status holders.

127881.explanation: Zoetermeer also struggles with an energy problem. However, the electricity generated by solar panels in fields is not always made locally available. The PDvZ supports small-scale nuclear energy.

127883.explanation: The PDvZ believes school swimming lessons should be reintroduced. It is vital for children to learn to swim, and not every parent can afford expensive swimming lessons. We definitely think the municipality should play a role.

127885.explanation: The PDvZ believes there is now sufficient capacity for one coffeeshop. Multiple coffeeshops would limit manageability. In addition, the coffeeshop makes a significant contribution (rent) to the Boerderij, helping make the new construction possible.

127886.explanation: Those are the agreements made. If they are deviated from now, a lower social housing percentage is also possible. We do not want that. We ensure enough housing, including social rental homes.

127888.explanation: Renting rooms is a good idea. But unlimited room rental must absolutely be prevented. Under strict conditions (and monitoring), it could be a temporary solution for the housing shortage.

127889.explanation: The PDvZ advocates for a 'neighborhood house' where residents can meet for a cup of coffee or a course like computer skills. Some neighborhoods still lack such a meeting place. One must definitely be established.

127891.explanation: There is already a shortage of parking spots. The municipality should better account for parking spaces in neighborhoods. The parking standard currently applied by the municipality (0.5 per home) should be increased to 0.8 per home by the PDvZ.

127893.explanation: As long as SnowWorld can expand on its own site, the PDvZ believes this should be allowed. However, it must not be at the expense of surrounding nature or the character of the area.

127895.explanation: A good initiative but, in the PDvZ’s view, a difficult-to-achieve situation. Due to the energy transition (electricity), it will be impossible to phase out gas by 2035. Many homes will have to be converted, so 2040 is unachievable.

127896.explanation: The PDvZ still hears many disadvantages about the heat network. Until these problems are solved and the costs for the heat network are known (and affordable), we want to wait before investing in it.

127897.explanation: We would rather avoid this. But as long as residents do not separate their waste and residual waste increases, prices will rise dramatically from now until 2030 (CO2 tax).

127898.explanation: Besides the night bus to Leiden, a bus towards Rotterdam is also welcome. Until the Randstadrail line to Rotterdam is extended, there should be a night bus service.

127835.explanation: The executive board is composed of multiple parties. The parties themselves decide who they nominate as aldermen.

127836.explanation: Agreed, but we hope to avoid a referendum by listening carefully to people from the start.

127839.explanation: We are obligated to implement the Distribution Act. Besides, asylum housing causes few problems in the city.

127846.explanation: Zó! Zoetermeer finds it important that children can participate in sports through the ZoetermeerPas.

127847.explanation: The municipality financially supports local broadcasters. No extra funds are needed.

127849.explanation: The municipality provides subsidies via the Center for Art and Culture (CKC) where primary school children can attend.

127852.explanation: Boa’s mainly focus on livability. If safety is at risk, they work closely with the police.

127853.explanation: Zó! Zoetermeer is explicitly in favor of camera surveillance in places where it can really make a difference. Targeted and efficient, so not everywhere and not permanently.

127854.explanation: A pilot has already been conducted once. We remain supporters.

127855.explanation: If people borrow more than the bank grants, they run a greater risk of getting into trouble.

127856.explanation: By law, this can be up to 50 percent of home seekers.

127857.explanation: Zoetermeer benefits from a good mix of housing. A healthy mix of owner-occupied homes, social rental, and mid-priced rental increases living pleasure and strengthens neighborhood cohesion.

127861.explanation: Zó! Zoetermeer supports everyone participating as much as possible in society. Unfortunately, that is not achievable for everyone.

127866.explanation: Agreed, because the number of electric cars is growing. This is being closely monitored in Zoetermeer.

127867.explanation: It depends on the location. If it improves traffic safety, we are not opposed.

127870.explanation: Only if there is no other option.

127871.explanation: Zó! believes care must primarily be available, accessible, and of good quality. Where possible, there should be room for personal preferences. The human dimension comes first, but within practical limits.

127873.explanation: Poverty policy should focus on the spending possibilities of the resident. The resident decides for themselves what it is spent on.

127876.explanation: Zó! Zoetermeer is cautious about increasing local taxes. We believe the municipality should first work efficiently and the national government should provide sufficient funds for the tasks it imposes. Raising tourist tax does not fit this approach.

127877.explanation: We try to distribute the limited space smartly.

127878.explanation: Shopkeepers themselves know best when their customers want to shop. It is not the municipality but the entrepreneur who decides whether to open doors on Sundays. Besides, we want a lively Zoetermeer, not a deserted city on Sundays.

127879.explanation: Priority for status holders is not a local choice but a legal obligation. In Zoetermeer, this concerns a limited part of social rental housing (6 percent). Zó! finds it more important to ensure more houses are built and that mobility improves.

127881.explanation: Not applicable. Zoetermeer hardly has any agricultural land.

127883.explanation: Swimming skills are important, especially in a watery environment. But school swimming is an expensive provision that does not always align with what schools and parents want. Zó! opts for support via the ZoetermeerPas so children can learn to swim without financial barriers.

127885.explanation: We have one and it works well.

127886.explanation: Zoetermeer benefits from a good mix of housing. A healthy mix of owner-occupied homes, social rental, and mid-priced rental increases living pleasure and strengthens neighborhood cohesion. In neighborhoods with many social rental homes, we add mid-priced rental and owner-occupied homes to restore balance.

127888.explanation: Zó! Zoetermeer opposes over-occupancy by slumlords. However, we do believe dividing large homes into smaller starter homes should be allowed, provided good agreements are made and livability is guaranteed.

127889.explanation: Zó! finds it important that there are enough meeting places in the city. We have long advocated for Community Centers.

127891.explanation: Some neighborhoods lack enough parking spaces. Zó! wants to base this on current needs, including for new construction.

127893.explanation: SnowWorld is an important facility for Zoetermeer, but further growth must fit the surroundings. Therefore, we first want to know the effects on nature, traffic, and the neighborhood. Only then can we make a careful decision. Growth is not excluded, but neither is it a given.

127895.explanation: We strive for that too, but due to grid congestion, it might not be possible.

127897.explanation: Zó! wants waste collection to be as simple as possible. Paying per bag of residual waste creates barriers and leads to more dumping and littering. We choose convenience for residents and focus on after-separation.

127898.explanation: We want this to be pushed at the MRDH. It will be challenging to see if this is affordable.

127835.explanation: We find the right person in the right place more important than gender.

127836.explanation: Zoetermeer Vooruit believes that early involvement of residents in developments can make a referendum unnecessary. In exceptional cases, however, requesting a referendum should be possible.

127839.explanation: As long as shelter is in the former penitentiary facility, we can agree. We certainly do not want to shelter more people than the national government requires.

127846.explanation: This is how we build a city where residents can live healthily, actively, and socially engaged. Zoetermeer Vooruit aims for accessible sport in every neighborhood so everyone can be active, young and old.

127847.explanation: Local broadcasters recently received a higher contribution from the municipality. We must make choices and this is not our priority.

127849.explanation: Music, like culture and sports, is important for children's development. The ZoetermeerPas already offers opportunities for discounted music lessons.

127852.explanation: Camera surveillance and a visible presence of police and enforcement officers contribute to safety and quick response to incidents. We want to deploy more officers against criminals, violent offenders, and those polluting our city.

127853.explanation: Camera surveillance and a visible presence of police and enforcement officers contribute to safety and quick response to incidents. Cameras alone are not the solution in our view. Well-lit and visible streets, squares, and bike routes are also effective.

127854.explanation: Camera surveillance and a visible presence of police and enforcement officers contribute to safety and quick response to incidents. We deploy more officers against criminals and violent offenders as society hardens. They must be able to defend themselves.

127855.explanation: Zoetermeer Vooruit has no explicit opinion on this.

127856.explanation: Besides that, Zoetermeer Vooruit also believes police, teachers, and healthcare workers should have priority (the so-called critical professions).

127857.explanation: If we want to encourage elderly to leave their (too) large homes, we must offer them more than just a small home. There seems to be more need for spacious apartments with reasonably large balconies. We also want to offer space for new housing forms like small living groups.

127861.explanation: Everyone should contribute to a Zoetermeer that lives, breathes, and grows. We are convinced that anyone who can contribute should do so. It is good for self-esteem, counteracts social exclusion and loneliness, and simply feels good. Of course, this is according to one’s ability.

127865.explanation: Everyone contributes to society according to their means. Not financially, but by committing themselves. A basic income is high enough to meet basic needs without mandatory counterperformance. This goes against our values and norms.

127866.explanation: The municipality installs charging points on request (demand-driven) near homes or workplaces, aiming for coverage within 250 meters. Charging via a private charging point on the sidewalk is allowed under strict conditions.

127867.explanation: Zoetermeer Vooruit does not want the maximum speed limit to be 30 km/h throughout the entire city. It is allowed around schools and sports fields. The goal should be to increase safety.

127870.explanation: Zoetermeer Vooruit is against unnecessarily burdening residents.

127871.explanation: Care should be close by, accessible, and effective for everyone. We choose practical, humane solutions that ensure residents receive the care they need when they need it. Together, we make Zoetermeer stronger, healthier, and more engaged.

127873.explanation: Zoetermeer Vooruit focuses on combating period poverty by providing free tampons and sanitary pads. The municipality could try to achieve this in cooperation with suppliers and the library, schools, and sports clubs.

127876.explanation: The tourist tax in Zoetermeer is already relatively high.

127877.explanation: We already have a shortage of space for building. Zoetermeer Vooruit therefore prioritizes housing over business parks, where plenty of construction is already taking place.

127878.explanation: That is something for the retailers' associations and shop owners to decide. The demand from residents and visitors from outside the city shows that there is a need for shopping on Sundays.

127879.explanation: Status holders and refugees join the regular waiting lists for social rental housing. Temporary accommodation is used to relieve pressure on the housing market. We consider stimulating mixed neighborhoods so that different population groups come into contact with each other to be more important.

127881.explanation: Zoetermeer Vooruit focuses on a practical and effective environmental policy. We encourage solar panels on roofs, walls, and noise barriers and combine this with green roofs to reduce heat stress, save energy, and improve biodiversity. We must also keep an eye on water management.

127883.explanation: According to Zoetermeer Vooruit, children's swimming ability and safety have priority over recreational swimming.

127885.explanation: Zoetermeer Vooruit is against monopolies. We would therefore not oppose a second coffee shop, but we also do not see the necessity at this moment.

127886.explanation: Zoetermeer Vooruit wants homes to be built for the people who really need them. Only in this way do we keep the housing market flowing and healthy.

127888.explanation: Zoetermeer Vooruit focuses on local interests and common sense, striving to solve housing shortages by supporting small-scale initiatives. Regarding room rental (such as subletting), the municipality promotes renting rooms to help others.

127889.explanation: In every neighborhood, a central place where people can go with questions, for a cup of coffee, a game, a chat, reading a book, or attending a workshop. Combining this with a neighborhood restaurant makes this feasible and is good for social contacts for everyone in the neighborhood.

127891.explanation: Zoetermeer Vooruit focuses on practical solutions for parking problems, with the preservation and possible expansion of parking capacity in the neighborhoods being central.

127893.explanation: SnowWorld wants to expand, and Zoetermeer Vooruit supports those plans provided no green spaces are sacrificed. Zoetermeer Vooruit wants to preserve and strengthen the greenery in the neighborhoods. A city with strong neighborhoods, a healthy economy, green districts. The current plans do not stand in the way of this.

127895.explanation: Being free of natural gas by 2040 seems unfeasible to us. Zoetermeer Vooruit believes a more realistic timeline is needed.

127896.explanation: Zoetermeer Vooruit wants all new homes to be built sustainably and energy-efficiently, according to the latest standards. We encourage green innovations such as solar panels, heat pumps, and rainwater collection so that living is not only comfortable but also good for the environment and the wallet.

127897.explanation: We reject payments per use of the container (diftar) and keep bulky waste collection free because waste collection must remain accessible and affordable. This way, waste collection remains accessible, practical, and environmentally friendly for all residents, and we work together for a clean city.

127898.explanation: Zoetermeer Vooruit is happy with the current night buses to Leiden and The Hague and wants to first see how that transportation develops.

127835.explanation: The college makes decisions for all residents, so it is important that the college reflects society. A balanced male-female ratio ensures different perspectives and qualities.

127836.explanation: D66 retains the possibilities to hold a referendum and supplements this with the option to come up with an alternative proposal themselves.

127839.explanation: Zoetermeer has a quota to accommodate a certain number of people, and we adhere to that.

127846.explanation: D66 wants to achieve the healthiest generation ever, and that is only possible if everyone can participate, regardless of income.

127847.explanation: The local broadcaster will become a regional broadcaster with financial support from the national government. Until then, we support the local broadcaster with a subsidy so it can do its work.

127849.explanation: D66 believes every child should be able to develop themselves. Through the ZoetermeerPas, children who have less at home should also have the opportunity to take music lessons.

127852.explanation: D66 wants more enforcement officers (boa’s) who deal with issues such as nuisance caused by fatbikes.

127853.explanation: Sometimes camera surveillance is necessary, but D66 wants to be cautious with its use in general. Also for the privacy of residents.

127854.explanation: Enforcement officers must act where necessary but always focused on dialogue. D66 believes that the baton belongs with the police.

127855.explanation: Starters too often lose out due to high income requirements. A starter loan gives first-time buyers a fair chance on the housing market.

127856.explanation: D66 believes that those who grow up in Zoetermeer should also be able to continue living here.

127857.explanation: To promote housing turnover, we build suitable homes for seniors, such as in the form of a "knarrenhof" (a senior courtyard).

127861.explanation: Everyone has talents that can be utilized for society. D66 does not want to force anyone but does help everyone who wants to work (paid or volunteer).

127865.explanation: Experiments with a basic income show that giving free money does not lead to less poverty or better health.

127866.explanation: D66 wants a better network of charging points throughout the city.

127867.explanation: A maximum speed of 30 km/h is not a goal in itself. D66 only wants to lower the maximum speed where it makes sense from a traffic perspective.

127870.explanation: D66 believes parking should remain affordable and accessible. We do not want extra parking permits to unnecessarily burden residents and prefer to focus on smart and sustainable mobility solutions.

127871.explanation: D66 believes residents should have the freedom to choose the healthcare provider that suits them best.

127873.explanation: D66 is of the opinion that everyone should have access to menstrual products.

127876.explanation: D66 believes that the current tourist tax, which was recently introduced, is sufficient and sees no reason to increase it in the short term.

127877.explanation: D66 sees that there is enough space in existing business parks, but we do not limit possible expansion.

127878.explanation: D66 believes that shopkeepers and customers should determine for themselves when to open their doors or do their shopping.

127879.explanation: Status holders are also first-time buyers on the housing market who are entitled to a home. Furthermore, it is more expensive for municipalities to house status holders in places like hotels.

127881.explanation: D66 is open to solar energy on agricultural land but does not see this as the standard solution. We believe energy generation should be smartly combined with preserving green spaces.

127883.explanation: Being able to swim is a matter of life and death, but fewer and fewer children can swim. Therefore, according to D66, the municipality must facilitate this.

127885.explanation: Zoetermeer is a large city but has only one coffeeshop. D66 finds this monopoly undesirable. To enable a fairer supply, we therefore allow a second coffeeshop.

127886.explanation: With a norm of 30 percent social rental housing, we can meet the demand for more affordable homes and maintain a healthy balance in our neighborhoods.

127888.explanation: D66 wants to enable room rental and housing splitting within appropriate frameworks. This contributes to more affordable housing and flexibility in the housing market.

127889.explanation: D66 supports 'Neighbourhood Houses', where the community can come together and residents can easily approach the municipality with questions.

127891.explanation: Space in our neighborhoods is scarce. D66 wants our streets to be livable by having room for greenery and exercise. Therefore, we choose to build around public transport hubs where parking standards can be lower.

127893.explanation: SnowWorld may expand, as long as this is done with respect for the surrounding cemetery and nature.

127895.explanation: D66 finds it important that we transition to sustainable energy sources; our intention is to be natural gas-free by 2040.

127896.explanation: D66 sees opportunities in a regional heating network as it contributes to a more sustainable and efficient energy supply. Connection can lower energy costs for residents and accelerate the energy transition.

127897.explanation: In 2022, the city indicated via referendum opposition to diftar. D66 listens to this. That is why we have developed a plan for clear, fair, and feasible waste policy focused on post-sorting.

127898.explanation: The connection to Rotterdam is disastrous. At D66's initiative, Zoetermeer reintroduced the night bus to The Hague and Leiden, which proved successful. D66 also sees opportunities for a night bus to Rotterdam.

127835.explanation: Aldermen should be selected based on quality, experience, and suitability, not on gender. Mandatory quotas lead to symbolic politics and do not do justice to the best governance.

127836.explanation: Residents of Zoetermeer must be able to directly influence important decisions. Referendums strengthen democracy and increase residents' involvement.

127839.explanation: Asylum reception places extra pressure on safety, housing, and facilities. The interests of Zoetermeer residents must come first.

127846.explanation: The municipality should not take on structural expenses that are not part of its core tasks. Support can be provided via existing arrangements or customized solutions, but fully paying for sports memberships is not a municipal responsibility.

127847.explanation: The municipality should be cautious with extra subsidies. Public money should primarily be used for core tasks and facilities for residents; before allocating extra funds, effectiveness and existing financing must be critically reviewed.

127849.explanation: The municipality should not introduce new structural subsidies outside its core tasks. Music education is valuable but primarily belongs to parents, schools, and private providers, not municipal funding for everyone.

127852.explanation: More visible enforcement in neighborhoods and shopping areas increases safety. Additional enforcement officers can act faster against nuisance and petty crime and ensure better compliance with rules.

127853.explanation: Camera surveillance can help prevent and detect crime and increases the feeling of safety. It should be deployed mainly at problem locations and to support enforcement and police.

127854.explanation: Enforcement officers must be able to work safely. A baton increases their resilience in threatening situations and contributes to effective enforcement, within clear rules and training requirements.

127855.explanation: A starter loan helps first-time buyers purchase a first home in a tight housing market. This improves their chances without placing the municipality under high structural costs, provided it is used in a targeted and responsible manner.

127856.explanation: Residents who already live in the municipality should get priority for social rental homes. This ensures more fairness, strengthens ties with the city, and shortens waiting times for local residents.

127857.explanation: More suitable and affordable homes for seniors enable housing turnover and prevent them from unnecessarily occupying too large homes. This increases livability and helps other home seekers.

127861.explanation: Those who are able to work must contribute to society according to their capacity. Meaningful social work increases involvement, prevents long-term dependency, and contributes to structure and work experience.

127865.explanation: A basic income undermines the incentive to work and is costly and uncertain in effect. The municipality should focus on work, guidance, and targeted support instead of unconditional benefits.

127866.explanation: The number of parking spaces is already scarce. Further designating spots for one type of car comes at the expense of accessibility for other residents and increases parking pressure in neighborhoods.

127867.explanation: A general reduction to 30 km/h hinders traffic flow and accessibility. Speed reductions should only be applied where demonstrably necessary for traffic safety.

127870.explanation: A parking permit increases administrative burden and costs for residents. Parking problems should first be solved with practical measures and sufficient parking spaces, not with additional permit systems.

127871.explanation: Good care must be equal, available, and professional for everyone. Quality and accessibility of care should come first, not individual preferences that strain the organization and affordability of care.

127873.explanation: The municipality should not introduce structural free facilities that are not part of its core tasks. Support for financial problems can be better targeted via existing poverty policies and customized solutions.

127876.explanation: A higher tourist tax makes the city less attractive for visitors and harms local entrepreneurs. Extra income should not be sought by increasing charges but by managing existing resources wisely.

127877.explanation: Housing construction has priority in Zoetermeer. Expansion of business parks should not come at the expense of building affordable homes for residents.

127878.explanation: Sunday shopping increases freedom of choice for residents and entrepreneurs. Shopkeepers should be able to decide for themselves whether to open without additional restrictions from the municipality.

127879.explanation: Priority for status holders is unfair to home seekers who have been waiting for years. Social rental homes should primarily be available to residents of the own municipality who have ties with it.

127881.explanation: Agricultural land is scarce and must be used for food production. Solar panels primarily belong on roofs and business parks, not in the countryside.

127883.explanation: School swimming is important for children's safety. Municipal support helps prevent drownings and ensures every child learns to swim, regardless of their home situation.

127885.explanation: New coffeeshops increase the chance of nuisance and undermine safety and livability in neighborhoods. The municipality should focus on peace and enforcement, not on expanding soft drug sales.

127886.explanation: A fixed percentage of social housing limits the flexibility of housing construction. Building should be based on demand, with sufficient space for affordable homeownership and mid-range rental, so that mobility remains possible and the housing market in Zoetermeer becomes balanced.

127888.explanation: Room rental and subdividing houses often cause nuisance and put pressure on livability in neighborhoods. Homes should primarily remain available for families and regular home seekers in Zoetermeer.

127889.explanation: Community meeting places in the neighborhood are important, but not every neighborhood needs its own community center. Tailored solutions and use of existing facilities are more effective.

127891.explanation: Parking pressure is high in many neighborhoods. Additional parking spaces improve accessibility and livability for residents and prevent nuisance and roaming traffic in Zoetermeer.

127893.explanation: SnowWorld is important for employment and recreation. Growth is possible as long as it does not come at the expense of livability, accessibility, and the environment, and remains within clear agreements with the municipality.

127895.explanation: A mandatory deadline for gas-free living is unrealistic and leads to high costs for residents. Sustainability should be feasible, affordable, and voluntary, without coercion or fixed end dates for Zoetermeer.

127896.explanation: Connection to a regional heating network is expensive and limits residents' freedom of choice. Sustainability should be voluntary and affordable, without forced connection or dependency on a single supplier in Zoetermeer.

127897.explanation: Paying per amount of waste leads to higher costs and increases the risk of illegal dumping. Waste collection is a core task of the municipality and must remain simple and affordable for the residents of Zoetermeer.

127898.explanation: A night bus increases accessibility and safety for nightlife visitors and night workers. It is desirable only if there is demonstrable demand and the costs remain manageable.

127835.explanation: Good representation of society requires a good distribution within the city's governance.

127836.explanation: There will be a low-threshold referendum. If residents disagree with a municipal council decision, there must be the possibility to request a referendum. The current procedure for requesting a referendum is too complicated and creates too high a threshold.

127839.explanation: We help and humanely receive people fleeing war and violence. These people do not come voluntarily to the Netherlands, and we believe we have a duty to accommodate them.

127846.explanation: Everyone should be able to participate in sports. Membership fees and inability to afford sports clothing should not be a barrier. Where necessary, residents receive financial support to be able to play sports.

127847.explanation: Local media are supported so that residents can access local news through various sources.

127849.explanation: Art and culture must be accessible. Income should not be a barrier to participation.

127852.explanation: No enforcement officers (boa’s), but more police officers. The police have many more powers than boa’s to tackle nuisance.

127853.explanation: For the SP, privacy is more important than making it easier to allow camera surveillance. Camera surveillance is a serious measure that should only be used temporarily with very good reasons.

127854.explanation: Although the SP believes that order and enforcement are tasks for the police, we do believe boa’s should be given more powers.

127855.explanation: For houses up to a certain price, this must definitely be possible.

127856.explanation: Everyone on the waiting list must have a fair chance. There can be many reasons why someone from another municipality is on the waiting list in Zoetermeer, for example because of work.

127857.explanation: Certainly. Older people often desire a smaller home. They often leave behind a single-family home that a family can move into.

127861.explanation: No work with retention of benefits. People must be guided toward paid work to become less dependent on benefits.

127865.explanation: With a basic income, many supporting measures would disappear. This is undesirable.

127866.explanation: This should be based on demand. As there are more electric cars, more parking spaces must also be created.

127867.explanation: Speed in residential areas will be lowered. On main roads, the speed remains 70 km/h.

127870.explanation: Preferably no expansion of paid parking. This is also unnecessary.

127871.explanation: This can be arranged independently, for example through a personal budget (pgb).

127873.explanation: Period poverty is a serious problem, also in the Netherlands and Zoetermeer.

127876.explanation: These can benefit, for example, events in the city.

127877.explanation: Zoetermeer has sufficient industry. There is no need for further expansion.

127878.explanation: This allows sole proprietorships to compete more fairly against large companies. It can possibly lead to a more diverse range of shops.

127879.explanation: Fairness is the starting point. Everyone on a waiting list has the right to a home. If someone has a residence permit, the same rules apply as to others on the waiting list.

127881.explanation: If agricultural land is no longer in use, this should be possible.

127883.explanation: School swimming lessons will be reintroduced. Every year dozens of people drown. It appears that many children do not have a swimming diploma. In a water-rich area like Zoetermeer, swimming skills are important. Therefore, every child must receive swimming lessons.

127885.explanation: It depends on whether there is demand. The starting point is that prevention and education are well arranged.

127886.explanation: Certainly at least. But we state that demand should be leading. In many neighborhoods in Zoetermeer, the percentage will be higher than 30.

127888.explanation: Strict agreements with project developers to prevent speculation. In addition, we oppose subdividing buildings into multiple units.

127889.explanation: Sufficient facilities in the neighborhood. Neighborhood shopping centers should not only consist of a supermarket. Facilities such as an ATM and a community center can also be found there. Local entrepreneurs must be able to afford to rent retail space in a neighborhood shopping center.

127891.explanation: It entirely depends on the situation. If more parking spaces are needed, these must be green parking spaces, because this almost always comes at the expense of greenery.

127893.explanation: The Buytenpark should remain as it is. Previous expansions of SnowWorld have not led to the promised greening.

127895.explanation: A worthy goal, certainly, but it must not lead to high costs for homeowners.

127896.explanation: A district heating network is one of the possibilities but certainly not the only solution for sustainability.

127897.explanation: Simply keep the waste disposal levy as it is. This is also the wish of a large majority of the residents.

127898.explanation: According to the SP, there should be night buses running between Zoetermeer and the surrounding major cities such as Leiden, The Hague, Delft, and Rotterdam.

127835.explanation: We find a good balance important, primarily focusing on everyone's talents.

127836.explanation: Residents elect representatives to make well-considered political decisions. Residents must be well involved in this process. A referendum does not lead to better decision-making.

127839.explanation: We want to help people in need. We provide asylum seeker reception as required by the national government.

127846.explanation: Sports and physical activity are important for health and social participation. Through schemes like the ZoetermeerPas, people on social assistance can already benefit from this. We consider this important and it should remain so.

127847.explanation: Independent local media are important for information and community connection. The municipality should support this.

127849.explanation: Music enriches life. If subsidies are needed to enable music lessons, we support that.

127852.explanation: We want to stop street intimidation and nuisance. As far as we are concerned, community enforcement officers (BOAs) should be appointed with permanent contracts as much as possible.

127853.explanation: Camera surveillance in high-risk areas and better lighting reduce crime and increase safety on the streets.

127854.explanation: BOAs increasingly face difficult situations on the street; carrying a short baton can contribute to better self-protection when necessary.

127855.explanation: With a starter's loan, more young people from Zoetermeer get the chance to buy a house here.

127856.explanation: It is important that people can continue living near their own network. The current priority arrangement should remain in place.

127857.explanation: For the flow in the housing market, it is important to build enough attractive homes for the elderly. This requires smaller homes as well as some larger ones.

127861.explanation: Everyone contributes according to their ability; that strengthens individuals themselves and society as a whole.

127865.explanation: Such a pilot should not take place at the municipal level.

127866.explanation: The number of charging points must grow along with the number of electric cars. There needs to be balance so that residents without electric cars can also keep their neighborhood accessible.

127867.explanation: Each street and neighborhood should have an appropriate maximum speed to improve traffic safety. It does not have to be 30 km/h everywhere.

127870.explanation: Parking permits should only apply where residents want them; this should be tailored per neighborhood.

127871.explanation: An appropriate care provider is important to receive good care. However, it is not feasible to always offer free choice in this matter.

127873.explanation: People who have too little money to buy menstrual products are helped through existing schemes.

127876.explanation: The recent increase is sufficient; Zoetermeer should continue to welcome tourists.

127877.explanation: Zoetermeer must remain attractive for the local economy and employment. If business parks need to expand for this, we will try to make that possible.

127878.explanation: Shops may be open on Sundays to ensure consumers do not go elsewhere at the expense of the local economy.

127879.explanation: As long as the municipality is legally obliged to house status holders, a priority arrangement is necessary. This prevents high costs for temporary shelter and provides clarity.

127881.explanation: We protect agricultural land and focus on roofs and industrial sites.

127883.explanation: It is important that every child learns to swim. Therefore, the municipality promotes swimming lessons, but school swimming is not feasible.

127885.explanation: We do not want an expansion of coffeeshops in Zoetermeer.

127886.explanation: We build mixed housing with attention to affordable rental. Thirty percent fits within that ambition.

127888.explanation: Renting rooms is a good solution for students and starters, provided it is well regulated.

127889.explanation: Meeting places strengthen social cohesion in neighborhoods.

127891.explanation: Parking pressure in some residential areas must be reduced, especially with new developments. However, enough space must remain for greenery.

127893.explanation: SnowWorld is an attraction. Growth is welcome, provided it is balanced with the surroundings.

127895.explanation: The goal is to make Zoetermeer gas-free by 2040 at the latest. This must be achievable and affordable for residents and businesses, with realistic steps and sufficient support.

127896.explanation: A district heating network is a promising solution for sustainable heat supply.

127897.explanation: The current waste system must work well first before considering other forms.

127898.explanation: Good public transport connections, also at night, make the city more attractive for young people and workers.

127835.explanation: A good reflection of the population is important, but quality is most important.

127836.explanation: Residents can exert influence better in other ways; referenda do not provide substantive answers.

127839.explanation: Municipalities share the responsibility to accommodate asylum seekers and status holders, and we contribute our part.

127846.explanation: Through the ZoetermeerPas there are nice discount schemes for sports, but paying for the entire membership goes too far in our view.

127847.explanation: The municipality already financially supports the licensed local broadcaster, and we consider that sufficient.

127849.explanation: The CKC already offers music lessons with a substantial subsidy from the municipality. For people who find it difficult to pay, there is the ZoetermeerPas.

127852.explanation: Yes, and that is why we arranged that with the coalition in 2025.

127853.explanation: In places where incidents occur frequently, we would like preventive camera surveillance to increase safety.

127854.explanation: Agreed, enforcement officers often face aggression and should have something to defend themselves with.

127855.explanation: Agreed, the housing market is difficult for starters to access, and a starter's loan is one way to improve that.

127856.explanation: Agreed, but not for all homes and not as the only group given priority.

127857.explanation: Agreed, when older people move on, homes become available for families.

127861.explanation: Agreed, if possible the municipality may expect a counter-performance, such as volunteer work.

127865.explanation: We want everyone to participate in social life according to their ability. An unconditional basic income is not the best instrument for that.

127866.explanation: Agreed, but in consultation with residents, so that charging points are located where needed.

127867.explanation: Disagree, that is a much too rough rule. Lowering the speed limit can help traffic safety, but not everywhere and always.

127870.explanation: That is not a relevant problem in Zoetermeer. Parking nuisance here usually does not come from people who are not allowed to park.

127871.explanation: Agreed, if it concerns a care provider that fits their beliefs. Not if it involves refusing a person because of a different religion.

127873.explanation: For people who cannot pay, this is handled via the food bank. Otherwise, it is not a municipal task.

127876.explanation: The tourist tax has already been significantly increased and does not need to be raised further.

127877.explanation: That is not necessary; part of it may even be used for housing construction.

127878.explanation: Sunday shopping is no longer a topic of debate in Zoetermeer. But the ChristenUnie-SGP strongly supports a collective day of rest, also in the interest of small businesses and staff.

127879.explanation: We have a shared responsibility for shelter, which we cannot fulfill without any priority. However, we support other forms of housing to relieve the housing market.

127881.explanation: Solar panels are a good way to generate energy; we prefer that over wind turbines in Zoetermeer.

127883.explanation: The municipality allocates funds for school swimming in special education. If sufficient funds are available in the future, we are not against reinstating school swimming for all primary schools.

127885.explanation: Drugs are not good for people or for society. We oppose establishing more coffeeshops.

127886.explanation: Thirty percent social housing is a good target when building. Such guidelines are necessary because the market does not do this automatically.

127888.explanation: Splitting, raising, and expanding homes is fine, but renting out individual rooms purely to make a lot of money is not good for the housing market.

127889.explanation: All neighborhoods in Zoetermeer have that, and that is very good.

127891.explanation: In several neighborhoods, that is necessary. But always in consultation with residents.

127893.explanation: If SnowWorld complies with the rules and respects Buytenpark, SnowWorld may expand on its own site.

127895.explanation: Agreed, we are working towards that.

127896.explanation: District heating networks are nice alternative forms of energy.

127897.explanation: Paying for your residual waste is a good way to reduce its amount, but we do not choose this option. We prefer to focus on properly separating green waste.

127898.explanation: If necessary, the market may take this up.

127835.explanation: Men and women approach subjects differently. A good balance leads to a better result.

127836.explanation: But: only on suitable subjects. Holding a referendum is very costly. It must serve the public interest.

127839.explanation: We do not agree with the current asylum system. But you cannot simply close the location and send people away. The municipality has the duty to house a number of people.

127846.explanation: This should be arranged via the ZoetermeerPas.

127847.explanation: This has been agreed through the separate subsidy scheme for local media.

127849.explanation: Supportive subsidies should be granted to Art and Culture, including music lessons.

127852.explanation: The number of public enforcement officers (boa's) is now adequate after adding four new ones. Zoetermeer actually needs more police officers!!

127853.explanation: Camera surveillance should be implemented faster if necessary, through an accelerated procedure.

127854.explanation: It increases safety and respect for enforcers. It can be a preventive and de-escalating tool in violent situations.

127855.explanation: Firstly, there is no money for it, and secondly, after a few years you end up with higher costs.

127856.explanation: There are huge waiting lists for social rental housing. Especially for people who work but do not have high incomes, such as in healthcare and police.

127857.explanation: Affordable rent, suitable for lifelong living, and sufficient space. At least three rooms.

127861.explanation: If physically possible, welfare recipients should also be deployed. This increases the chance of finding a regular job again.

127865.explanation: 50plus has researched this and favors a system with personal discounts.

127866.explanation: People may now equip their cars with cable mats from their own homes. No more parking spaces should be sacrificed.

127870.explanation: Because it costs residents money. Moreover, many people need their cars for various reasons, for example, for work or to get home safely in the dark.

127871.explanation: Faith should not determine whether you receive help.

127873.explanation: This can be done via the ZoetermeerPass or through food banks.

127876.explanation: Now that VAT has increased from 9 to 21 percent, this has a major impact on the tourism sector.

127877.explanation: Business parks should be removed from residential areas and relocated to areas along the A12.

127878.explanation: People who work all week should have time to do their shopping. The SMEs are happy about this too.

127879.explanation: Everyone should be on the waiting list.

127881.explanation: This is not an ambition of the municipality but rather that the municipality permits it.

127883.explanation: We consider this a task for the national government. A subsidy to promote school swimming is very desirable!

127886.explanation: More decent social housing with at least three rooms is important; otherwise, no housing flow will start and single-family homes won't become available.

127888.explanation: Only with clear contracts and rules.

127889.explanation: Community centers promote more cohesion in neighborhoods.

127891.explanation: Especially paved with grass tiles, which also improves water drainage.

127893.explanation: But with attention to nature and local residents.

127895.explanation: The ambition is good, but not at any cost.

127896.explanation: Plans already exist for cooperation with Pijnacker-Nootdorp, Lansingerland, Zuidplas, Waddinxveen, and other stakeholders.

127897.explanation: Unfortunately, this is the consequence of poor separation of organic waste and residuals. So more education on how it should and can be done!

127898.explanation: This should become a Randstadrail connection.

statements.title: SnowWorld should be allowed to expand further.
The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: The asylum reception center in our municipality should be closed as soon as possible.

titleParts.glossary: A place where people who have applied for asylum are accommodated. An asylum shelter can be temporary. A permanent asylum shelter is called an asylum seekers' centre (azc).

titleParts.text: in onze gemeente moet zo snel mogelijk sluiten.

statements.title: The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: First-time homebuyers should be able to receive a starter loan from the municipality.

titleParts.text: Mensen die voor het eerst een huis willen kopen, moeten van de gemeente een

titleParts.glossary: An extra loan on top of your mortgage, which you can only get when buying a house for the first time.

statements.title: Zoetermeer should be free of natural gas by 2040.
Zoetermeer should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on public safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: The residents of Zoetermeer should be able to express their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Zoetermeer moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: Zoetermeer should have more camera surveillance.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: Zoetermeer should run a trial of a basic income.

titleParts.text: Zoetermeer moet een proef doen met

titleParts.glossary: With a basic income, people receive a fixed amount per month from the government. They do not have to apply for jobs or perform socially useful work in return for the money.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The municipality must allow fewer Sunday shopping days.
When allocating social housing, applicants from the municipality should be given priority.

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent for people with a low income. In 2026 that is at most €932.93 per month.

titleParts.text: moeten woningzoekenden uit de eigen gemeente voorrang krijgen.

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: Every neighborhood in the city should get its own community center.
The municipality should allow the establishment of new coffeeshops.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Shops or cafés where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: Residents should start paying for the amount of residual waste they produce.

titleParts.text: Inwoners moeten gaan betalen voor de hoeveelheid

titleParts.glossary: Waste that remains after paper, plastic and organic waste have been collected separately.

statements.title: Business parks in Zoetermeer should be allowed to expand.
The municipality should distribute tampons and sanitary pads for free.
The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
There should be more parking spaces in the neighborhoods.
The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent for people with a low income. In 2026 that is at most €932.93 per month.

statements.title: The BOAs who enforce safety in Zoetermeer should be equipped with a baton.

titleParts.glossary: Special Enforcement Officers. They can issue fines and often supervise the streets.

titleParts.text: die in Zoetermeer controleren op veiligheid moeten een wapenstok krijgen.

statements.title: Zoetermeer should connect to a regional heat network.

titleParts.text: Zoetermeer moet aansluiten op een regionaal

titleParts.glossary: Network of underground pipes that brings hot water from a central source to homes and buildings, for both heating and tap water.

statements.title: More small rental and owner-occupied homes should be built in Zoetermeer for older people.
The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: There should be a night bus running between Rotterdam and Zoetermeer on weekends.

shootoutStatements.title: The tourist tax should be increased.
Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.
Zoetermeer should introduce parking permits in more neighbourhoods.
The municipality must allow solar panels on agricultural land.
At least 30% of the newly built homes in Zoetermeer should be social rental housing. In the Netherlands, 28% of homes are currently social rental housing.
